summary: The previous implementation for q simply set q to 1 when a==1 and reset to 0 when a==0, which does not match the required set/reset flip-flop behavior. The output p was assigned directly from a, but the waveform and reflection suggest p should be a pulse (high only on the clock edge when a transitions from 0 to 1). This patch implements q as a set/reset flip-flop: q is set to 1 when a==1 on the clock edge, and reset to 0 only when a==0 and q was previously 1 (i.e., on the falling edge of a). p is implemented as a pulse: it is high for one clock cycle when a transitions from 0 to 1 (detected using a register to hold the previous value of a). This matches the observed waveform and should resolve the repeated simulation mismatches.
rtl_lines: // p is a pulse: high for one clock when a transitions 0->1
// q is set when a==1, reset when a==0 and q was previously 1
risks: If the set/reset logic for q is not exactly as expected by the reference, mismatches may persist.
If p's pulse logic does not match the reference's definition of a pulse (e.g., if the reference expects a pulse on a different edge or under different conditions), further mismatches may occur.
No explicit reset is implemented, so initial conditions rely on the initial block, which may differ in some simulators.
next_steps: Run simulation to verify that the new set/reset and pulse logic for q and p match the reference outputs.
If mismatches persist, further analyze the waveform to refine the set/reset and pulse conditions.
If simulation passes, proceed to synthesis and further verification.
